article_title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness

article_items.title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
article_items.description: Learn how agent harnesses transform AI models into autonomous work engines. Explore core components: filesystems, sandboxes, and memory.
article_items.excerpt: Learn how agent harnesses transform AI models into autonomous work engines. Explore core components: filesystems, sandboxes, and memory.
article_items.summary: The model contains the intelligence and the harness makes that intelligence useful. From a Model's Perspective There are things we want an agent to do that a model cannot do out of the box. The practical impact: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness Break down complex objectives: Planning tools let agents decompose tasks, track progress, and adapt as they learn Delegate work in parallel: Spawn subagents for independent subtasks, each with isolated context TLDR: Agent = Model + Harness. key_points: The model contains the intelligence and the harness makes that intelligence useful.
From a Model's Perspective There are things we want an agent to do that a model cannot do out of the box.

The Anatomy of an Agent Harness Break down complex objectives: Planning tools let agents decompose tasks, track progress, and adapt as they learn Delegate work in parallel: Spawn subagents for independent subtasks...

Orchestration Logic (subagent spawning, handoffs, model routing) Hooks/Middleware for deterministic execution (compaction, continuation, lint checks) There are many messy ways to split the boundaries of an agent...
The main idea is that we want to convert a desired agent behavior into an actual feature in the harness.
Models (mostly) take in data like text, images, audio, video and they output text.
rag_angles: what changes for developers or users
what the launch makes easier, faster, cheaper, or more accessible
what limitation or rollout detail readers should watch next
rag_rules: Lead with the actual product, model, or API name.
Explain what changed before explaining why it matters.
Anchor credibility with dates, model names, pricing, availability, benchmarks, regions, or partners when the source provides them.
State the rule, complaint, restriction, or risk plainly.

article_items.title: Intelligent radiology workflow optimization with AI agents | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: Many healthcare organizations report that traditional worklist systems rely on rigid rules that ignore critical context, radiologist specialization, current workload, fatigue levels, and case complexity. This creates a persistent challenge: radiologists cherry-pick easier, higher-value cases while avoiding complex studies, leading to diagnostic delays and increased costs. Research across 62 hospitals analyzing 2.2 million studies found
